title: spec-authoring
description: The STANDARD a spec is authored and maintained against — not a gate cell, but the build-against-the-standard map spec-author works toward (the spec-quality rubric in dev-kit-corpus is the gate that ships it; this is what you aim for). Each dimension bridges to the gate dimension that mechanically checks its floor AND the spec-council lens that adversarially pressure-tests its judgment — author against the dimension, the gate checks the mechanical part, a critic hunts the rest. The author never mints the signal that validates its own spec.
the_split: GUIDANCE (this file, in the skill) is what good authoring is; the GATE (rubric.system.spec-quality, in dev-kit-corpus) is what ships. A dimension here maps to a gate dimension + a council lens; the three are one standard seen from the author's, the machine's, and the adversary's seat.

dimensions.what_good_is: The spec IS a mini-skill: front-matter with `name` (== the cell slug) and a `description` that states the actual intent + scope (not 'a spec for X'); a brief body that reads top-to-bottom; the embedded ```json contract block; and references/ for depth a 30-second reader does not need. The human surface and the machine contract agree.
dimensions.smell: A bare json contract with no intent surface; a description that restates the title; a name that disagrees with the contract cell.

dimensions.what_good_is: The description + brief capture the principal's ACTUAL want — what changes for them and why — grounded in the intent source, nothing invented. Because the spec layer is upstream of every other, drift here multiplies; this is the highest-leverage dimension.
dimensions.smell: A 'should' with no owner; a term used two ways; an assumption stated nowhere; a want the source never expressed.

dimensions.what_good_is: Every acceptance criterion is a checkable predicate — an executable `check` or a binding to a validated `rubric_cell`. Zero prose-only criteria. A criterion is what `done` means; a hope is not a criterion.
dimensions.smell: 'It should feel fast'; 'works well'; a criterion no run could ever pass-or-fail.

dimensions.what_good_is: The necessary criteria, the edge cases, and the failure modes are present — not just the happy path. Every boundary answers: what crosses it, in what form, under whose authority, and what happens when it fails.
dimensions.smell: Only the success path specified; an error path, a limit, or an empty-state left to the builder's guess.

dimensions.what_good_is: Non-goals are explicit and the boundary is held. The spec authorizes exactly its intent — no unbounded 'and also…', no scope the principal never asked for.
dimensions.smell: No non-goals; a non-goal that contradicts a criterion; scope creep smuggled into the brief.

dimensions.what_good_is: When decomposed, satisfying the children ENTAILS satisfying the parent (a property under the partial order, not a surface coverage count) AND the carving is the RIGHT one — the structure that tunnels the build well, not merely a covering. The decomposition is a typed delta (cells + dependency-ordered tickets), never prose.
dimensions.smell: An orphan parent criterion no child covers; a covering decomposition that carves along the wrong seam; a child binding no rubric.

dimensions.what_good_is: The acceptance criteria cannot be satisfied WITHOUT satisfying the intent — the upstream analogue of a reward-hack-resistant rubric. A criterion verifies a property under transformation, not an extensional surface a builder can game.
dimensions.smell: A criterion a trivial or adversarial implementation passes while missing the point; 'tests green' as the whole bar.

dimensions.what_good_is: The spec carries its provenance and is kept true across its life — corrected only by a deliberate, LEDGERED `validated → regenerating` transition motivated by operating evidence, never a silent edit. A spec is managed, not authored once and abandoned.
dimensions.smell: A validated spec quietly edited; a correction with no ledger evidence; a stale spec whose dependents were never re-flagged.

how_to_use: AUTHOR / UPDATE: hill-climb the spec against these dimensions (evaluator-optimizer; generate, a separate critic scores against this standard, revise the weakest), then submit to the gate. REVIEW: the gate checks the gate_dimension floors mechanically; the spec-council runs the council_lens for each. A dimension green on the gate but weak on its lens is a real finding — the floor passed, the judgment didn't.
